sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 8.26 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.8)

statement.latex: We call a variety passable\/ if there exists an unrefinable chain of its subvarieties which is well-ordered by inclusion. For example, every variety generated by its finite groups is passable --- this is an easy consequence of (H.\,Neumann, Varieties of groups, Berlin et al., Springer, 1967, Chapter~5). Do there exist non-passable varieties of groups? { A.\,V.\,Kuznetsov} Yes, there do (M.\,I.\,An...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
